Namespace: primesieve

primesieve

A primesieve, full implementation.
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 1

Members

(static, constant) error :number

Variable to hold error number
Type:
  • number
Default Value:
  • 0
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 271

(static) fill

Grows the sieve to the size given by the argument. Does not reduce the size or increment the sieve, it just produces a new sieve of the given size.
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 695
See:
  • primesieve.raiseLimit
  • primesieve.grow

(static, constant) primesizelimit :number

The guard limit of the primesieve.
Currently set to one megabibyte, which can hold more than half a million primes.
Type:
  • number
Default Value:
  • 8388608
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 40
See:
  • primesieve.raiseLimit

Methods

(static) factor(prime) → {array}

Returns factors of argument
Returns all factors in an array, for a different format primesieve.primeDecomposition
Parameters:
Name Type Description
prime number positive integer < 253
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 385
See:
  • primesieve.primeDecomposition
Returns:
list of all factors in increasing order or undefined in case of an error
Type
array

(static) grow(alot) → {bool}

Grows the sieve to the size given by the argument. Does not reduce the size or increment the sieve, it just produces a new sieve of the given size.
Parameters:
Name Type Description
alot number positive small integer
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 667
See:
  • primesieve.raiseLimit
Returns:
undefined in case of an error
Type
bool

(static) isPrime(prime) → {bool}

Checks if the given number is a prime.
Might need a couple of seconds for larger primes.
Parameters:
Name Type Description
prime number positive integer < 253
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 337
Returns:
or undefined in case of an error
Type
bool

(static) isSmallPrime(prime) → {bool}

Checks if the given number is a small prime (must be in the sieve)
For larger numbers see primesieve.isPrime
Parameters:
Name Type Description
prime number positive small integer
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 309
See:
  • primesieve.isPrime
Returns:
or undefined in case of an error
Type
bool

(static) nextPrime(prime) → {number}

Returns prime greater than argument
Parameters:
Name Type Description
prime number positive small integer
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 477
Returns:
Next prime or undefined in case of an error
Type
number

(static) precPrime(prime) → {number}

Returns prime greater than argument
Parameters:
Name Type Description
prime number positive small integer
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 501
Returns:
Preceding prime or undefined in case of an error
Type
number

(static) primeDecomposition(prime) → {array}

Returns factors of argument as tuples (prime, exponent)
For a different format see primesieve.factor
Parameters:
Name Type Description
prime number positive integer < 253
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 426
See:
  • primesieve.factor
Returns:
list of all factors in increasing order as tuples of the form (prime, exponent) packed into an array or undefined in case of an error
Type
array

(static) primePi(prime) → {number}

Number of primes up to argument (counts primes in sieve)
Parameters:
Name Type Description
prime number positive small integer
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 525
Returns:
Number of primes or undefined in case of an error
Type
number

(static) primePiApprox(prime) → {number}

Number of primes up to argument (approximation independant of sieve)
Parameters:
Name Type Description
prime number positive small integer
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 560
Returns:
Number of primes or undefined in case of an error
Type
number

(static) primeRange(low, high) → {array}

Produces an Array holding all primes between low and high
Parameters:
Name Type Description
low number positive small integer
high number positive small integer
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 580
Returns:
Primes between low and high or undefined in case of an error
Type
array

(static) primes(prime) → {array}

Produces an Array with the primes between zero and the given argument
Parameters:
Name Type Description
prime number positive small integer
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 626
Returns:
Primes between zero and the given argument
Type
array

(static) raiseLimit(raise) → {bool}

To avoid unexpected surprises this primesieve has an in-build limit for the size of the primesieve of one megabyte. Does not lower the limit.
Parameters:
Name Type Description
raise number size of the new maximum in bits
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 704
Returns:
undefined in case of an error
Type
bool

(static) sieve() → {primesieve}

Returns the raw prime-sieve. It might be an Array or a TypedArray, so please check before use.
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 723
Returns:
the raw primesieve
Type
primesieve

(static) strerror() → {string}

Function to convert an error number into a human readable string
Source:
  • primesieve.js, line 278
Returns:
Summary of the error
Type
string
